---
title: "Day 32 — Padding, masks, and truncation: the bugs that never raise"
subtitle: "Monday, 24 August 2026 · Week 5 · Pretrained models · Study day · 3-4 h"
---

::: {.day-nav .kind-study}
[← Day 31](day-31.qmd) · [All 50 days](../timeline.qmd) · [Day 33 →](day-33.qmd)
:::

::: {.why}
A wrong attention mask does not throw an exception; it quietly lets the model attend to padding and hands you a model two points worse for no visible reason, which is why this gets a whole day instead of a footnote.
:::

## What you should be able to do tonight

- You can show, with numbers from your own run, that padding a short sequence without passing its attention mask changes that sequence's logits, and say by how much.
- You can write a `Dataset.map(..., batched=True)` call over 20,000 abstracts and explain why setting `padding='max_length'` inside it defeats the point of `DataCollatorWithPadding`.
- You can name the `max_length` you will use for the project, taken from your own token-length distribution, and the percentage of abstracts it truncates.

## Tasks <span class='task-total'>200 min</span>

::: {.task-list}
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [55 min]{.task-min} Work chapter2/5 (Handling multiple sequences) and chapter2/6 (Putting it all together), reproducing the padding experiment yourself: run one sentence alone, then the same sentence padded in a batch with and then without `attention_mask`, and print all three logit vectors.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [40 min]{.task-min} Read chapter2/7 and chapter2/8 (Optimized Inference Deployment), then take the chapter2/9 quiz. Section 8 is the one that tells you what your Day 35 latency numbers will be made of, so read it as preparation rather than trivia.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [60 min]{.task-min} Read chapter3/1 and chapter3/2 (Processing the data) and write the pipeline you will reuse for the rest of the bootcamp: load a JSONL into a `Dataset`, `map` a tokenise function with `batched=True` and no padding, then hand `DataCollatorWithPadding` to the loader. Save it as `project/data_pipeline.py`, not as loose notebook cells.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [45 min]{.task-min} Tokenise your abstract sample (extend it to 2,000 if the export is quick), plot the token-length histogram, mark the 95th and 99th percentiles, and pick your `max_length` from the plot rather than defaulting to 512. Record the truncation rate at your choice.
:::
:::

## Resources

- [HF LLM Course ch2 §5 - Handling multiple sequences](https://huggingface.co/learn/llm-course/en/chapter2/5) [course]{.res-kind}
- [HF LLM Course ch2 §6 - Putting it all together](https://huggingface.co/learn/llm-course/en/chapter2/6) [course]{.res-kind}
- [HF LLM Course ch2 §7 - Basic usage completed](https://huggingface.co/learn/llm-course/en/chapter2/7) [course]{.res-kind}
- [HF LLM Course ch2 §8 - Optimized Inference Deployment](https://huggingface.co/learn/llm-course/en/chapter2/8) [course]{.res-kind}
- [HF LLM Course ch2 §9 - Quiz](https://huggingface.co/learn/llm-course/en/chapter2/9) [course]{.res-kind}
- [HF LLM Course ch3 §1 - Introduction](https://huggingface.co/learn/llm-course/en/chapter3/1) [course]{.res-kind}
- [HF LLM Course ch3 §2 - Processing the data](https://huggingface.co/learn/llm-course/en/chapter3/2) [course]{.res-kind}
- [Hugging Face Datasets docs](https://huggingface.co/docs/datasets) [docs]{.res-kind}
- [Hugging Face Transformers docs](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers) [docs]{.res-kind}

## Deliverable

::: {.deliverable}
`day32_plumbing.ipynb` with the three-way padding experiment printed as numbers, `project/data_pipeline.py` with a working `map` plus `DataCollatorWithPadding` chain you can import tomorrow, and a token-length histogram with your chosen `max_length` drawn as a vertical line and the truncation rate stated in the title.
:::

## Checkpoint

::: {.checkpoint}
You tokenise with `padding='max_length', max_length=512` inside `Dataset.map` and also pass `DataCollatorWithPadding` to the Trainer. Nothing errors and the metrics look fine. What did that cost you, and roughly how much, given the histogram you just plotted?
:::

## Prepare for tomorrow

Ten minutes tonight so Day 33, Tuesday, 25 August 2026 starts clean. This is what the evening email sends you.

::: {.task-list .prep}
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Register for a free NCBI API key (Account -> Settings -> API Key Management). It takes three minutes and raises E-utilities from 3 to 10 requests per second.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Read only the ESearch and EFetch examples in the E-utilities Quick Start (NBK25501) - skip the rest of the chapter.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Paste the PubMed query you settled on into `project/query.txt`, so tomorrow opens with a script to run rather than a decision to make.
:::
:::
